regions.text: Low-melting-point metals are used in fire controls. A fire melts the metal, which triggers an electric system.
This opens ceiling outlets that spray water to put out the fire. (Illustration: Fire melting a link and sprinklers activating).
A fire system may be expensive, but it's much cheaper than the cost of a new building. It is cheap insurance.
melt: become liquid. ceiling: inner roof. cheap: low price for value. FUTURE: will melt. PRESENT: melt(s). PAST: melted.
